steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: To the left of the blank we have the compound noun object “hotels and ______” of the gerund “visiting.” To the right begins the subordinate clause “as it grew in scope and influence…”, which provides essential timing information for the main clause that follows.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A semicolon is needed to separate the two independent clauses on either side of the blank: the first describes the brothers' original purpose, and the second (beginning with "as it grew in scope and influence") is a complete clause about how the guide changed.

restaurants (no punctuation) creates a run-on, the remaining alternative inserts an extra "and" that doesn't fit, and the remaining alternative produces a comma splice.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
